summary.intent: Develop and maintain the `httpd-crate` branch by merging main repeatedly, resolving conflicts, addressing code review comments from greptile on PR #465, and fixing test/CI failures.
summary.outcome: Branch was successfully merged with main multiple times, all greptile review comments were fixed and resolved across multiple rounds, a squashed PR #465 was created after git history rewrite complications, and most test failures were fixed (sandbox failures identified as pre-existing on main).

repo: The `httpd-crate` branch moves TLS/gateway functionality from `moltis_gateway` to `moltis_httpd` crate — rustls provider init, browser warmup, and session event enrichment all had to migrate.

The `moltis-gateway` crate's `PreparedGatewayCore` uses `..` destructure patterns that silently drop fields like `_proxy_shutdown_tx` and `browser_tool_for_warmup` — these must be explicitly extracted and stored.
Nightly rustfmt enforces grouped `use` blocks — separate `use moltis_gateway::...` and `use moltis_httpd::...` must be merged into a single `use { ... }` block.
The `PreparedGateway` struct in httpd has private fields; making `_proxy_shutdown_tx` public (`pub _proxy_shutdown_tx`) is required for the swift-bridge crate to access it.
Metrics collection interval in httpd is intentionally 30s (not 10s) — a merge conflict resolution accidentally set it to 10s.
The sandbox E2E tests (`sandboxes.spec.js`) fail when Docker/container runtime is unavailable — these are pre-existing failures unrelated to branch changes.
The `/ws` route alias alongside `/ws/chat` is required so node-add generated commands work correctly.
Session event enrichment (label, model, preview, channel binding) was simplified/lost during the gateway→httpd migration and needed to be ported back.
git-filter-repo removes the `origin` remote as a safety measure after rewriting history — must re-add manually before pushing.

code.finding: rustls::crypto::ring::default_provider().install_default() must be called in prepare_gateway (not start_gateway) so both the direct and swift-bridge/embedded startup paths initialize the TLS provider.
PreparedGateway struct needs pub _proxy_shutdown_tx field to keep the trusted-network proxy alive; dropping it via .. pattern kills the proxy immediately.
start_browser_warmup_after_listener needs to accept browser_tool_for_warmup as a parameter — it was extracted from the PreparedGatewayCore destructure and threaded through BannerMeta.
systemd ExecStart arguments (--node-id, --host, --token, display name, working dir) must be quoted to handle spaces; launchd plist values need xml_escape for &, <, > characters.
Duplicate /api/sessions route registrations were introduced — axum panics at startup on duplicate routes.
Merge conflict resolution incorrectly chose !(isMain || isCron) instead of main's !isMain, hiding the delete button for cron sessions — test expects cron sessions to be deletable.
Local MAX_LIFETIME constant shadowed by module-level MAX_BROWSER_INSTANCE_LIFETIME — the local one was unused dead code introduced during merge.

workflow: git filter-repo rewrites the entire history including the base branch commits, breaking the shared ancestor with origin/main — the fix is to create a new branch from origin/main and apply the diff as a squashed commit.
When filter-repo rewrites history, GitHub closes open PRs because the branch SHA association is lost — a new PR must be created.
gh CLI requires `gh repo set-default` to be configured before most operations work in a worktree context.
Greptile review comments are fetchable via GitHub API (`gh api repos/.../pulls/.../comments`) and resolvable via the review threads API.
After squashing a multi-commit branch into one commit for a PR, subsequent `git push` may fail due to upstream tracking mismatch — use `git push --set-upstream origin <branch>`.

friction: git filter-repo removed the origin remote automatically, which was unexpected and caused the PR to close when force-pushing.
The rewritten history had no common ancestor with origin/main, requiring manual branch reconstruction (new branch from origin/main + cherry-picked diff) instead of a simple force push.
gh pr reopen failed because filter-repo had rewritten commit SHAs, making GitHub unable to associate the branch with the old PR.
moltis-agents was not a dependency of the httpd crate, causing a compile error when trying to use AgentTool type — required adding the dependency to Cargo.toml.
Multiple rounds of greptile comments across the same PR required repeated fetch-fix-resolve cycles.
A merge conflict resolution in session-header.js introduced a regression (hiding cron delete button) that wasn't caught until E2E tests ran.
PreparedGatewayCore private field visibility blocked swift-bridge compilation and required making the field pub.
open_items: Sandbox E2E tests (sandboxes.spec.js) fail when container runtime is unavailable — identified as pre-existing on main but not fixed; may need environment setup or test skipping logic.
The squashed single-commit PR loses the detailed per-commit history of the httpd-crate refactor — consider whether the PR description adequately documents the changes.

The `cron session shows delete button in more controls` test fix reverted to main behavior (!isMain) — if cron sessions should eventually be non-deletable, this needs a separate intentional change with a test update.
